reasoning: This is one direction of the biconditional proved by the fully verified `combiner_request_ids_not_contains` lemma. Fresh rid not being in the aggregate set is correct by design.
The other direction of the same fully verified biconditional. The fold-over-choose correctly captures all combiners — this is the intended semantics.

For a singleton map `{nid -> cs}`, the universally quantified freshness condition reduces to checking just that one node's queue. This follows directly from the definition of `combiner_request_id_fresh`.
`CombinerState::Ready.queued_ops()` returns `Seq::empty()`, so `seq_to_set` of it is the empty set. Adding an empty set to the aggregate changes nothing. Correct by design.
